title: Product Design and Development Manufacturing
description: Evaluates advanced understanding of product design processes, design techniques, environmental considerations, quality deployment, robustness, and technology in design.
subject: DSC2370 - Operations Management

questions.question: Which statement best explains why product design decisions have long term strategic consequences for an organization?
options: They determine short term marketing campaigns
They lock in cost, quality, and manufacturability early in the life cycle
They are easily reversible during production
questions.answer: They lock in cost, quality, and manufacturability early in the life cycle
questions.rationale: Early design decisions constrain future operational performance.

questions.question: Why is idea generation considered a broad and inclusive activity rather than a purely technical one?
options: Because it focuses only on engineering feasibility
Because valuable ideas can emerge from multiple internal and external sources
Because it eliminates the need for market analysis
Because it occurs after feasibility studies
questions.answer: Because valuable ideas can emerge from multiple internal and external sources
questions.rationale: Ideas originate from customers, employees, suppliers, and competitors.

questions.question: A feasibility study primarily acts as a filter in the design process because it:
options: Prevents technically attractive but unviable ideas from consuming resources
questions.answer: Prevents technically attractive but unviable ideas from consuming resources
questions.rationale: Feasibility screens ideas before heavy investment.

questions.question: Performance specifications are most critical because they:
options: Describe how the product will be marketed
Translate customer needs into measurable design targets
questions.answer: Translate customer needs into measurable design targets
questions.rationale: They guide designers on what the product must achieve.

questions.question: Rapid prototyping contributes to better design outcomes mainly by:
options: Encouraging early testing and learning through fast failures
Reducing the need for pilot runs
questions.answer: Encouraging early testing and learning through fast failures
questions.rationale: Early failures are cheaper and more informative.

questions.question: Which aspect of design is primarily responsible for shaping customer first impressions?
questions.rationale: Appearance strongly influences attraction and appeal.

questions.question: Reliability in functional design is best described as:
options: Consistency of performance over a specified period under normal conditions
questions.answer: Consistency of performance over a specified period under normal conditions
questions.rationale: Reliability focuses on failure free operation over time.

questions.question: Why does modular design improve maintainability?
options: It reduces the need for user instructions
It allows entire units to be replaced independently
questions.answer: It allows entire units to be replaced independently
questions.rationale: Modules simplify repair and servicing.

questions.question: Poor production design most directly leads to:
options: Increased defects and higher production costs
questions.answer: Increased defects and higher production costs
questions.rationale: Difficult designs strain manufacturing capability.

questions.question: Standardization contributes to operational efficiency primarily by:
options: Reducing variety in components and processes
questions.answer: Reducing variety in components and processes
questions.rationale: Less variety simplifies production and purchasing.

questions.question: Design for manufacture emphasizes:
questions.rationale: DFM aligns design with manufacturing capability.

questions.question: Design for supply chain is most valuable because it:
options: Aligns product design with supplier capabilities early
questions.answer: Aligns product design with supplier capabilities early
questions.rationale: Early collaboration improves quality and speed to market.

questions.question: The main purpose of a pilot run is to:
options: Launch the product to all customers
Train the workforce and resolve remaining process issues
questions.answer: Train the workforce and resolve remaining process issues
questions.rationale: Pilot runs validate production readiness.

questions.question: Concurrent design reduces time to market primarily because:
options: Design decisions are made collaboratively and in parallel
questions.answer: Design decisions are made collaboratively and in parallel
questions.rationale: Parallel work reduces delays and rework.

questions.question: Failure mode and effects analysis focuses on:
questions.rationale: FMEA aims to prevent failures before they occur.

questions.question: Functional design emphasizes [dropdown_1], while production design emphasizes [dropdown_2].
questions.rationale: Each design focus addresses different success factors.

questions.question: Design for environment extends across [dropdown_1] and is guided by the principle of [dropdown_2].
questions.rationale: Environmental design considers people, planet, and profit.

questions.question: Quality function deployment primarily translates [dropdown_1] into [dropdown_2].
questions.rationale: QFD integrates the voice of the customer into design.

questions.question: Design that allows products to function over a wide range of conditions is known as design for ________.
questions.rationale: Robust design reduces sensitivity to variation.

questions.question: The assessment of environmental impact from raw material to disposal is called ________ assessment.
questions.rationale: This approach evaluates the full life cycle impact.

questions.question: Computer aided engineering allows testing designs ________ building physical prototypes.
questions.rationale: CAE simulates performance digitally.

questions.question: Which factors are explicitly considered in functional design?
questions.rationale: Functional design focuses on how the product performs and is used.

questions.question: Which practices support design for environment?
questions.rationale: These align with the three Rs principle.

questions.question: Which technologies directly support digital product design and analysis?
questions.rationale: These technologies enable digital design and manufacturing.

questions.question: Match each design stage with its primary purpose.
Match each design technique with its objective.
Match each life cycle phase with its focus.
Critically evaluate how concurrent design can reduce both cost and quality problems compared to a traditional sequential design approach.
Discuss the role of design for environment in achieving sustainable operations while remaining competitive.
Analyze how the integration of CAD, CAE, and CAD CAM technologies transforms the product design and development process.
